content: The Porch Light series began on a humid evening in NoDa, chasing the warm glow of a single porch bulb against the blue hour. Here is how the first frames came together and what they taught me about patience and available light.

content: I finally pulled three Porch Light frames for a limited fine-art print run. A look at the paper, the deckled edges, and why I print warm rather than neutral.

content: My current portrait kit is smaller than people expect. One body, two primes, and a folding reflector. Here is what stays in the bag and what got cut.

content: Warm skin tones do not happen by accident. My color workflow for the Porch Light look: white balance choices, split toning, and how I keep shadows from going green.
